DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF PETER CASTRO TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS AND TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION, AND FOR SANCTIONS, IS DENIED. IT IS UNDISPUTED THAT AFTER DEFENDANT FILED ITS MOTION TO COMPEL, PLAINTIFF PRODUCED DOCUMENTS AND HIS COUNSEL PROVIDED 11 DATES ON WHICH PLAINTIFF WAS AVAILABLE TO APPEAR FOR DEPOSITION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE PRO-TEM: MARGARET NIVER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Defendant Old Republic Title Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery, And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-28-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Old Republic Title Company's motion to compel Plaintiff Peter Castro to produce documents and to appear for deposition, and for sanctions, is DENIED. It is undisputed that after Defendant filed its motion to compel, Plaintiff produced documents and his counsel provided 11 dates on which Plaintiff was available to appear for deposition. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge Pro-tem: Margaret Niver; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2014 Continued To Apr-08-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-29-2014 Continued To Dec-24-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT OLD REPUBLIC TITLE COMPANY's Demurrer To First Amended Complaint - The court overrules the demurrer in its entirety. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the assignment of Ms. Grepo's claims at paragraphs 18-20 of the amended complaint. In Heritage Pacific Financial, LLC v. Monroy (2013) 215 Cal.App.4th 972, the allegations of the complaint were inconsistent with the promissory note, which was attached to the pleading. The Heritage court explained at 215 Cal.App.4th at 990: "We agree that the allegations in Heritage's SAC and the attached indorsement showed an assignment of Monroy's promissory note. However, the assignment of this contract right did not carry with it a transfer of WMC's tort rights. While no particular form of assignment is required, it is essential to the assignment of a right that the assignor manifests an intention to transfer 'the right.'" Here, the amended complaint has no such inconsistency and clearly states that plaintiff has the right to maintain the causes of action. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the breach of contract cause of action. Paragraph 58 of the amended complaint alleges the written contract (escrow instructions, closing statement, and e-mails) required defendant to pay off plaintiff's loan and paragraph 59 alleges that defendant failed to do so. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the contract terms according to their legal effect, i.e., plaintiff identifies the substance of the key terms that were breached. The fraud claims are sufficiently pleaded. Paragraphs 24-25 of the amended complaint allege details regarding who made the misrepresentations to Ms. Grepo regarding Saratoga Bancorp's authority to handle the payoff. Defendant complains that plaintiff does not plead more facts about defendant's fraudulent intent. A plaintiff need not particularize matters presumptively within the knowledge of the demurring defendant, however. See Turner v. Milstein (1951) 103 Cal.App.2d 651, 658. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
